DAMAS HOSPITAL SNF

CCN: 405023 · PONCE, PR 00717 · Ponce County

OverviewRatingsStaffingInspectionsDeficienciesPenaltiesOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesAll Data

Overview

Address
2213 PONCE BY PASS, PONCE, PR 00717
Phone
7878408686
Certified beds
25
Avg daily residents
18 (71% of beds filled)
Ownership
Non-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare
Medicare/Medicaid since
1988-09-08
Setting
Urban
4 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 4/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
Data not available.
— Not Available

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.99 3.87 4.98 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 4.19 0.68 4.17 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.81 0.86 0.80 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 0.00 2.34 0.00 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 4.99 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 1.61 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
4.13
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
3.25
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.11
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
13.6%
annual
RN turnover
11.1%
annual
Administrators departed
New facility — insufficient data.

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.37
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.00
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-03-28 10 10 0 104 1 104
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-05-17 20 13 7 204 1 204
Total weighted health score
129.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (38)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

3 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
34 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2025-03-28 2025-05-09
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. D 2025-03-28 2025-05-09
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2025-03-28 2025-05-09
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-03-28 2025-05-09
0805 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food prepared in a form designed to meet individual needs. F 2025-03-28 2025-05-09
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-03-28 2025-05-09
0813 Have a policy regarding use and storage of foods brought to residents by family and other visitors. F 2025-03-28 2025-05-09
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2025-03-28 2025-05-09
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. F 2025-03-28 2025-05-09
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. D 2025-03-28 2025-05-09
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-10-30 2025-02-07
0606 Not hire anyone with a finding of abuse, neglect, exploitation, or theft. D 2024-10-30 2025-02-13
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2024-10-30 2025-02-07
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. F 2024-10-30 2025-02-14
0940 Develop, implement, and/or maintain an effective training program for all new and existing staff members. F 2024-10-30 2025-02-07
0942 Ensure that staff members are educated on resident rights and facility responsibilities to properly care for its residents. F 2024-10-30 2025-02-07
0943 Give their staff education on dementia care, and what abuse, neglect, and exploitation are; and how to report abuse, neglect, and exploitation. F 2024-10-30 2025-01-04
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-05-17 2024-06-21
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. E 2024-05-17 2024-06-21
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2024-05-17 2024-06-21
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-05-17 2024-06-28
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. G 2024-05-17 2024-06-20
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. D 2024-05-17 2024-06-21
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-05-17 2024-06-20
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. D 2024-05-17 2024-06-20
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-05-17 2024-06-20
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly F 2024-05-17 2024-05-24
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2024-05-17 2024-06-20
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. F 2024-05-17 2024-06-26
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. E 2024-05-17 2024-05-28
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2023-04-13 2023-05-19
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. D 2023-04-13 2023-05-26
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted F 2023-04-13 2023-05-26
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. C 2023-04-13 2023-05-26
0741 Ensure that the facility has sufficient staff members who possess the competencies and skills to meet the behavioral health needs of residents. C 2023-04-13 2023-05-12
0802 Provide sufficient support personnel to safely and effectively carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service. C 2023-04-13 2023-06-05
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2023-04-13 2023-05-29
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. F 2023-04-13 2023-05-05
